title: Nataraja Lord of the Dance - Cleveland Museum of Art (28405168471).jpg
artist: Tim Evanson from Cleveland Heights, Ohio, USA
source_url: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Nataraja Lord of the Dance - Cleveland Museum of Art (28405168471).jpg
description:
Nataraja, Lord of the Dance, made about 1000 AD in Tamil Nadu, India On display at the Cleveland Museum of Art in Cleveland, Ohio. Made of bronze.

Nataraja depicts the Hindu god Shiva as the cosmic dancer who performs his divine dance (called the Tandavam) in order to destroy a weary universe and prepare for its renewal by the god Brahma, who starts the process of creation.

The ring of fire and the flame Nataraja holds are symbolic of destruction. The drum he holds represents the beat of time. His open hand indicates to his followers that they should not be afraid, for devotion to him (pointing to his raised foot) allows the believer to be liberated from this cycle of life and death.

He tramples on a dwarf, symbol of ignorance.
